read_plan.preview: Professional [[Adventurers]] operate systematically within infrastructure of specialized vendors, service providers, and institutions that enable expeditions to function. This support network is not romantic or adventuro
[[Specialized Equipment Vendors]] operate across three distinct models:
[[Intelligence Brokers and Cartographic Services]] provide critical operational foundation for expedition planning:

The numeric tier is the spell's **Flux handling requirement**. Each tier is roughly an order-of-magnitude jump in difficulty, capacity, stability, and precision. Every few tiers feels like a categorically different class
_Barely any Flux. The threshold most people never bother to formally test._
_A small, usable trickle. The first tier where deliberate spellwork is practical._
_The world average. The first tier where magic becomes genuinely useful in daily life._

The twenty members divide into three factions with distinct and occasionally competing goals. Alliances shift on specific votes but the factions themselves are stable — they represent genuine differences in what council

The largest faction, eleven members, organized around the council's defining long-term goal: the recovery and completion of whatever the pre-modern civilization in [[Equatorial Desert|Desert Zakros]] was building. The fa

Six members, organized around the straightforward goal of growing [[Auralis]]'s territory, population, and economic base. They are the most conventionally political faction — interested in trade routes, border security,

Three members who back [[Cassia]] with genuine loyalty: [[Maren Sollis]], an older woman who served under the previous queen and transferred her allegiance to the infant who survived her; [[Tovan Ashce]], a mid-tier cast

Auralis society leans toward a soft caste structure, where social standing is loosely tied to magical ability. This is not a rigid hierarchy of law — high-tier casters do not hold formal political office by virtue of the

Auralis embraces the full spectrum of [[Discipline|Disciplines]], including those that make other cultures uncomfortable. Experimentation with Mind and Soul disciplines is not openly banned and in certain scholarly or mi

The principal settlements of Auralis are [[Solhaven]] (the capital, built up coastal terraces on the western shore) and [[Emberfall]] (the interior research and agricultural center). Auralis has several additional port c
Solhaven approaches a million residents and is the most vertically stratified city in Solumora. Its terrace zones function as distinct social worlds stacked on each other.

The economy of Solumora follows a pattern familiar to anyone who has tried to survive in it: the things you need to stay alive are within reach, and everything that makes life worth living will cost you dearly.

The currency of Solumora is the **Watt**. [[Watts]] are not coins, notes, or physical objects. They are entries on a distributed ledger written into the ambient [[Flux]] field itself, verified by every transaction in the

A [[Birth Runes|birth rune]] is a personal [[Sigils|sigil]] that registers an individual within the ledger and serves as their financial identity. It is an address, not a container — the rune identifies who you are withi
The Solumoran economy splits cleanly along one line: survival is cheap, comfort is not.

Terravelle society values stability and efficiency above all. Where other cultures may chase magical prestige, Terravelle embraces the philosophy that the best spell is the one that accomplishes the task with the least e

Low to mid-tier casting is common and unremarkable. [[Grimoires]] here tend to favor Triangle and Square sigil shapes — fast, safe, reproducible, and affordable in [[Flux]]. [[Discipline|Disciplines]] with predictable fa

[[Terravelle]] and [[Auralis]] share a complicated relationship shaped by fundamentally opposed philosophies. Where Auralis pursues magical innovation and supremacy, Terravelle prizes restraint and function. The [[Equato

The principal settlements of Terravelle are [[Valdenmoor]] (the capital, in the central river valley), [[Ashford]] (the primary eastern port), and [[Greyveil]] (the northern waypoint at the edge of the settled belt). Doz

In [[Terravelle]], an adventurer is someone who has chosen persistent travel and practical service over stability. They move between settlements — sometimes over years, sometimes settling temporarily in one place — and t

In [[Auralis]], the role of adventurer has been systematically reframed over decades by [[The Council of Auralis|the Council]]. The base motivation remains intact — a desire to be beneficial, to do good — but what consti

The distinction between [[Terravelle]] and [[Auralis]] adventurers is not one of personal motivation — many [[Auralis]] adventurers genuinely believe they are doing good. The distinction is institutional. [[Terravelle]]

A [[Terravelle]] adventurer is often sustained by hope — the tangible evidence that their work matters, that people they help are better off, that their presence has created improvement. This is not sentimental. It is pr
[[Expedition Supplies]] are the minimum durable goods required for multi-day operations outside stable settlement infrastructure.
Thel Reach does not produce food, extract resources, or manufacture goods. It prepares people to cross the desert and profits from the knowledge that crossing without preparation kills.

Most permanent residents of Thel Reach are former desert guides, equipment specialists who have crossed multiple times, or families who provide support services (housing, meals, gear storage) to the transient traveler po

[[Halveth]] is the major border garrison and crossing coordination center. Thel Reach is the specialist preparation hub forty miles east where travelers go when they need services Halveth cannot provide at scale: multi-d

Thel Reach is the northern hub of an informal guide network that operates across the entire desert boundary. Guides based at the settlement maintain relationships with guides operating from other border settlements — the

top_pages.title: Rescue, Recovery, and Death Documentation Services

read_plan.section: Rescue, Recovery, and Death Documentation Services
read_plan.preview: Adventuring carries inherent mortality risk. Expeditions fail, explorers disappear, teams encounter situations beyond their capacity to manage. When casualties occur, survivors and family members face practical problems:

Private organizations deploying experienced personnel conducting search/rescue operations for missing or injured adventurers. Teams typically staff 4-8 specialists with mixed expertise: navigation (locating missing parti

[[Council of Auralis|Council]] and military maintain rescue programs for affiliated expeditions. Programs typically not available to unaffiliated adventurers unless contracted through formal service agreements.

Current [[Flux]] research is dominated by institutional networks in both kingdoms, each organized around distinct philosophical priorities and strategic orientations. **Contemporary military preparation is increasingly r

**[[Crestward Flux Institute]]** pursues cost reduction, parallel execution patterns, and methods to achieve higher-tier effects through coordinated low-tier practitioner teams. Leadership: [[Casten Miel]] ([[Control Tie

**[[Northern Terravelle Academy]]** emphasizes practical settlement-scale application and coordinated research on [[Rift Incursions|rift mechanics]]. The Academy operates independently of state control (through funding d

**Independent scholars** operate throughout both kingdoms, conducting collaborative research that neither kingdom prioritizes internally. [[Kedn Rast]] ([[Control Tier|T3]]) exemplifies this institutional model, conducti
**Crossing Risk Underwriters** are institutions and pooled-capital operators that price and absorb expedition-loss risk for desert crossings, ruin expeditions, and high-failure logistics contracts.
